TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Knights of Avalon Faction.

Translations.Comment: Direct translation. Noble knights of the legendary kingdom. Avalón is the standard Spanish name for Avalon.
Militia with swords. Espadachín conveys skilled sword-wielders, giving them a more martial feel than just 'sword militia'.
Untrained levies of peasants. Chusma suggests disorganized masses while aldeana denotes village folk. Conveys lack of discipline.
Militia armed with halberds. Alabarda is the Spanish word for halberd; alabardera refers to those who wield it.
Basic spear-wielding militia. Lancera denotes those armed with lances or spears, fitting the military hierarchy of basic infantry.
Young warriors training to become knights. Direct and clear, emphasizing their path to knighthood.
Light cavalry scouts. Cazadores montados emphasizes their mounted and hunting role as light cavalry and reconnaissance units.
Knights bound by sacred oaths. Juramentados conveys the solemnity of sworn vows and duty inherent to their order.
War dogs used in battle. Simple and direct, maintains their hunting/combat nature without military formality.
Skilled hunters serving as scouts and archers. Generic but effective; cazadores are versatile ranged combatants and scouts.
Siege weapon. Trebuchete is the direct Spanish adoption of the technical term, maintaining its technical precision in a fantasy wargame context.
Elite archers with longbows. Arqueros largos directly conveys 'long archers', emphasizing their specialized ranged prowess and status.
Elite royal guards. Guardia Real (Royal Guard) is the standard term, emphasizing their elite status and direct service to the crown.
Flying cavalry on pegasi. Caballeros Pegaso maintains the fantasy aesthetic while clearly identifying them as mounted knights of the skies.
Fantasy creature name (gryphon-horse hybrid). Hipogrifo is the standard Spanish term for this mythical hybrid, kept true to fantasy conventions.
Mythological winged horse. Pegaso is the standard Spanish adaptation of the classical mythological name, universally recognized in fantasy contexts.
Magical unicorn creature. Unicornio is the standard Spanish term, easily recognizable to Spanish-speaking fantasy enthusiasts.
Holy warrior maiden blessed by the divine. Doncella carries the noble and sacred connotation appropriate for a holy warrior, distinct from simple maiden.
Holy knight champion. Paladín is the direct Spanish adaptation of the universal fantasy term, maintaining recognition across all fantasy media.
